description:
<title>Synchronize Remote Log Files and Produce an Error Summary</title>

    <scenario>
        You are working as a junior log-analyst.  
        Your team keeps a “remote” drop-box of freshly generated application log files under  
        /home/user/remote_logs  
        while the authoritative, analyst-side archive lives under  
        /home/user/central_logs.  

        The senior analyst has asked you to:  
        1. Copy every new “*.log” file that exists in /home/user/remote_logs but is **not yet present** inside /home/user/central_logs.  
        2. Record exactly which files you transfer, in the moment you transfer them, inside a running text log called  
           /home/user/central_logs/sync_history.log.  
        3. After the copy, scan every log file whose name matches the pattern “*_20230910.log” now located inside /home/user/central_logs and count how many lines in each of those files contain the literal string “ERROR”.  
        4. Write a short, machine-checkable report containing those counts to  
           /home/user/analysis/error_summary_20230910.txt.  
    </scenario>

    <requirements>
        1. Only copy files that do **not** already exist in /home/user/central_logs. Do **not** overwrite any file that is already present.  
        2. Append one line to /home/user/central_logs/sync_history.log **for every single file copied**.  
           • The line must have this exact structure (single space separators):  
             [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] COPIED <filename> from remote  
           • Example (your actual timestamp will differ):  
             [2023-09-15 14:03:22] COPIED app1_20230910.log from remote  
           • Do not include any extra text.  
        3. The error summary file must contain exactly four lines, one per log file plus a final TOTAL line, in this precise format:  
           app1_20230910.log: <integer>  
           app2_20230910.log: <integer>  
           TOTAL: <integer>  
           Use a single colon followed by one space between the filename (or the word TOTAL) and the integer.  
        4. Place the summary file inside the directory /home/user/analysis (create the directory if it does not exist).  
        5. When finished, the directory /home/user/central_logs should now contain the newly copied files in addition to any older files that were already there, and /home/user/central_logs/sync_history.log must reflect the copy actions you performed.  
    </requirements>

    <hints>
        • Think of /home/user/remote_logs as a stand-in for an external server; any standard copy/synchronisation tool available in a normal Linux shell is acceptable.  
        • To make the sync-history lines easily verifiable, keep the exact text format shown above.  
        • A simple grep combined with wc can help count “ERROR” lines.  
        • Make sure paths and filenames match **exactly**; automated tests are case-sensitive.  
    </hints>

    <success_criteria>
        The automated checker will consider the task successful when:  
        • /home/user/central_logs now contains *both* app1_20230910.log and app2_20230910.log and their contents are byte-for-byte identical to the originals in /home/user/remote_logs.  
        • /home/user/central_logs/sync_history.log contains at least two new lines whose endings read  
          “COPIED app1_20230910.log from remote” and  
          “COPIED app2_20230910.log from remote”.  
        • /home/user/analysis/error_summary_20230910.txt exists and exactly matches the required four-line format and correct counts.  
    </success_criteria>
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory layout before the agent starts -->
    Directories:
        /home/user/remote_logs
        /home/user/central_logs
        (The directory /home/user/analysis does NOT exist yet.)

    File: /home/user/remote_logs/app1_20230910.log
    --------------------------------------------------------
    2023-09-10 08:00:01 INFO Startup complete
    2023-09-10 08:05:14 ERROR Failed to connect to database
    2023-09-10 08:07:55 INFO Retrying connection
    2023-09-10 08:08:02 ERROR Database connection timeout
    2023-09-10 08:09:10 INFO Shutdown initiated

    File: /home/user/remote_logs/app2_20230910.log
    --------------------------------------------------------
    2023-09-10 09:00:00 INFO Service started
    2023-09-10 09:15:22 WARN High memory usage
    2023-09-10 09:20:37 ERROR Out of memory
    2023-09-10 09:21:00 ERROR Could not allocate buffer
    2023-09-10 09:22:45 INFO Service restarted

    File: /home/user/central_logs/app1_20230909.log
    --------------------------------------------------------
    2023-09-09 07:59:59 INFO Previous day startup
    2023-09-09 08:10:10 ERROR Sample error
    2023-09-09 08:20:20 INFO Shutdown

    # sync_history.log might or might not exist initially; tests only check
    # that after the task it contains lines for the two new files.

    ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected artefacts AFTER a correct run (used by the grader):
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    1) /home/user/central_logs/app1_20230910.log
       – must be byte-for-byte identical to the file given above.

    2) /home/user/central_logs/app2_20230910.log
       – must be byte-for-byte identical to the file given above.

    3) /home/user/central_logs/sync_history.log
       – must contain at least the two lines (timestamps can vary):
         * COPIED app1_20230910.log from remote
         * COPIED app2_20230910.log from remote

    4) /home/user/analysis/error_summary_20230910.txt
       – must contain exactly:
         app1_20230910.log: 2
         app2_20230910.log: 2
         TOTAL: 4
name: task_000000_a4488933
